scenarios.title: Calculate flat rate margin with complete three-step process across multiple sub-margin groups
scenarios.intent: Verify flat rate margin calculation correctly executes all three steps: position selection by comparing long vs short market values per sub-group, applying flat margin rates, and applying margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant has positions identified per §3.2.2 across multiple sub-margin groups
Sub-group 1 positions: total absolute long market value is 30,000,000 HKD, total absolute short market value is 60,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 2 positions: total absolute long market value is 750,000 HKD, total absolute short market value is 0 HKD
Sub-group 3 positions: total absolute long market value is 0 HKD, total absolute short market value is 300,000 HKD
Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are: 12% for sub-group 1, 30% for sub-group 2, 55% for sub-group 3
Flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned per Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)
when: Step 1: System aggregates and compares total absolute market values of long vs short positions for each sub-margin group
Step 1: System includes sub-group 1 short positions (60,000,000 HKD), sub-group 2 long positions (750,000 HKD), and sub-group 3 short positions (300,000 HKD) in calculation
Step 2: System sums the product of absolute position market value and flat margin rate for each included position
Step 3: System applies the flat rate margin multiplier of 2
then: Sub-group 1: short positions included (60,000,000 HKD), long positions excluded because short absolute value is higher
Sub-group 2: long positions included (750,000 HKD), short positions excluded because long absolute value is higher
Sub-group 3: short positions included (300,000 HKD), long positions excluded because short absolute value is higher
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier equals (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000 HKD
assumptions: Flat margin rates are obtained from FieldType 3 as specified
Margin multiplier is obtained from Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)

evidence.quote: the total absolute short market value is higher than the total absolute long market value. Therefore, all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation while all long sub-group 1 positi...
Similarly for sub-group 2 and sub-group 3 to take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin when long and short market values are equal in sub-group
scenarios.intent: Verify behavior when total absolute long and short market values are equal for a sub-margin group
given: A clearing participant has positions in a sub-margin group
Total absolute long market value equals total absolute short market value (e.g., both are 50,000,000 HKD)
when: Flat rate margin calculation compares long and short market values for the sub-group
then: The system determines which positions to include based on the comparison result
Flat rate margin calculation proceeds to Step 2 with the determined positions
assumptions: The tie-breaking rule for equal long and short values is not explicitly stated in evidence; system behavior should be verified against HKEX specifications

evidence.quote: compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group

scenarios.title: Validate position identification and sub-margin group assignment
scenarios.intent: Verify that positions are correctly identified per §3.2.2 and assigned to appropriate sub-margin groups defined on HKEX website
given: A clearing participant submits position data for margin calculation
Positions include instrument IDs with quantities and market values
when: The system processes positions for flat rate margin calculation
then: Each position is identified per instructions in §3.2.2
Each position is assigned to the correct sub-margin group as defined on HKEX website
Absolute market values are calculated in HKD equivalent for long and short positions separately
assumptions: Position data includes all required fields for sub-margin group classification

evidence.quote: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately for each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2
each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website

scenarios.title: Calculate and round aggregated market-risk-component margin
scenarios.intent: Verify aggregated margin is correctly calculated from all market risk components and rounded up per the rounding parameter
given: Portfolio margin is 10,000,000
Flat rate margin is 15,180,000
Liquidation risk add-on is 266,865
Structured product add-on is 550,000
Corporate action position margin is 2,500,000
Holiday add-on is 18,433,039
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: Aggregated market-risk-component margin is calculated
Result is rounded up to nearest 10,000
then: Aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,929,904
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,930,000
assumptions: All market risk component values are provided as inputs

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on = 46,929,904
Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000)
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Rounding when aggregated margin is exact multiple of rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when aggregated margin is already an exact multiple of the rounding parameter
given: Aggregated market-risk-component margin is exactly 46,930,000
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: The system applies rounding to the aggregated margin
then: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 46,930,000
No upward adjustment is needed as value is already at rounding boundary
assumptions: Rounding up to nearest 10,000 means exact multiples remain unchanged

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000)
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000

scenarios.title: Validate all market risk components are present for aggregation
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required market risk components are present before calculating aggregated margin
given: A clearing participant has margin calculation request
when: The system validates input data for aggregated margin calculation
then: Portfolio margin value is present
Flat rate margin value is present
Liquidation risk add-on value is present
Structured product add-on value is present
Corporate action position margin value is present
Holiday add-on value is present
Rounding parameter from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is retrieved
assumptions: Missing components should trigger validation error before calculation

evidence.quote: Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components
Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on

scenarios.title: Calculate favorable MTM and derive net margin
scenarios.intent: Verify favorable MTM is correctly calculated and deducted from rounded aggregated margin to derive net margin
given: Market value portfolio is -300,550,000 HKD
Contract value portfolio is -287,850,000 HKD
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000 HKD
when: Favorable MTM is calculated using the formula
Net margin is derived by deducting favorable MTM from rounded aggregated margin
then: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) equals Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio = -12,700,000
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement with absolute value 12,700,000
Favorable MTM is zero in this case
Net margin equals Maximum(0, 46,930,000 - 0) = 46,930,000
assumptions: MTM requirement will be added after applying margin credit per §3.2.6.1

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit
Net margin = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)

scenarios.title: Net margin when favorable MTM equals rounded aggregated margin
scenarios.intent: Verify net margin is zero when favorable MTM exactly equals rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin
given: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000 HKD
Favorable MTM is 46,930,000 HKD
when: Net margin is calculated using Maximum(0, Rounded aggregated margin – Favorable MTM)
then: Net margin equals Maximum(0, 46,930,000 - 46,930,000) = 0
assumptions: This tests the boundary condition where the Maximum function returns zero

evidence.quote: Net margin = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)

scenarios.title: Validate portfolio market value and contract value inputs
scenarios.intent: Verify that portfolio market value and contract value are correctly aggregated for favorable MTM calculation
given: A clearing participant has positions with HKD equivalent contract values and market values
when: The system validates and aggregates portfolio values for favorable MTM calculation
then: Market valuePortfolio is aggregated from HKD equivalent market values
Contract valuePortfolio is aggregated from HKD equivalent contract values
Numbers are rounded off on position level
Multi-currency positions follow Appendix 4.6 calculation logic
assumptions: Position-level rounding is applied before aggregation

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
The aggregated value of HKD equivalent contract value and HKD equivalent market value. Numbers are rounded off on position level.
Please refer to Appendix 4.6 for calculation logic that involve multiple currencies in the portfolio

scenarios.title: Apply margin credit to calculate net margin after credit
scenarios.intent: Verify margin credit is correctly applied to net margin to derive net margin after credit
given: A clearing participant is granted a margin credit of 5,000,000 HKD
Net margin is 46,930,000 HKD
when: Net margin after credit is calculated using the formula
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (46,930,000 - 5,000,000)] = 41,930,000 HKD
assumptions: Margin credit value of 5,000,000 is the normal default value

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)] = Maximum [0 , (46,930,000 – 5,000,000)] = 41,930,000

scenarios.title: Net margin after credit when net margin equals margin credit
scenarios.intent: Verify net margin after credit is zero when net margin exactly equals margin credit
given: A clearing participant has net margin of 5,000,000 HKD
Margin credit is 5,000,000 HKD
when: Net margin after credit is calculated
then: Net margin after credit equals Maximum[0, (5,000,000 - 5,000,000)] = 0 HKD
assumptions: This tests the boundary where the Maximum function returns zero

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit value for clearing participant
scenarios.intent: Verify the correct margin credit value is retrieved and applied for each clearing participant
given: A clearing participant submits margin calculation request
when: The system retrieves the margin credit value for the participant
then: Margin credit is granted to each CP
Margin credit value is normally 5,000,000 HKD
Margin credit is applied for margin calculation
assumptions: Margin credit may vary per participant; system should retrieve participant-specific value

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation

feature: Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement from all components
scenarios.intent: Verify total MTM and margin requirement is correctly derived by adding net margin after credit to other risk components
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000 HKD
MTM requirement is 12,700,000 HKD
Position limit add-on is 487,332 HKD
Credit risk add-on is 12,000,000 HKD
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000 HKD
when: Total MTM and margin requirement is calculated
assumptions: All component values are derived from calculations under §3.2.5 and §3.2.6

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on = 41,930,000 + 12,700,000 + 487,332 + 12,000,000 + 600,000 = 67,717,332

scenarios.title: Total MTM and margin requirement when other risk components are zero
scenarios.intent: Verify total MTM and margin requirement equals net margin after credit when all other risk components are zero
given: Net margin after credit is 41,930,000 HKD
MTM requirement is 0 HKD
Position limit add-on is 0 HKD
Credit risk add-on is 0 HKD
Ad-hoc add-on is 0 HKD
when: Total MTM and margin requirement is calculated
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 41,930,000 + 0 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 41,930,000 HKD
assumptions: This tests the boundary where other risk components are absent or zero

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components

scenarios.title: Validate all risk components are present for total calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required risk components are present before calculating total MTM and margin requirement
given: A clearing participant has completed calculations under §3.2.5 and §3.2.6
when: The system validates input data for total MTM and margin requirement calculation
then: Net margin after credit value is present from §3.2.5.3
MTM requirement value is present from §3.2.5.2
Position limit add-on value is present
Credit risk add-on value is present
Ad-hoc add-on value is present
assumptions: Credit risk add-on may not be applicable upon launch of VaR Platform per footnote 46

evidence.quote: total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
Please note that the credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform
